tool_args.label: Capital Program Committee Meeting Book - February 2026
tool_args.raw_text: Capital Program Committee Meeting, February 2026, MTA Board and Committee Joint Meeting, January 28, 2026

payload.title: Capital Program Committee Meeting
payload.description: MTA Capital Program Committee meeting book for February 23, 2026, including Board meeting minutes from January 28, 2026, project status reports, safety reports, procurement actions, and committee work plan

tool_args.label: Jamie Torres-Springer, President of MTA C&D
Evan Eisland, Executive Vice President and General Counsel, C&D

payload.title: Executive Vice President and General Counsel, C&D

payload.description: MTA continuing to advance Second Avenue Subway Phase 2; tunneling contract awarded with approximately $700 million in labor savings and $1.3 billion in total identified savings
Formal kickoff of design and engineering for the Interborough Express project
Queens Interlocking is a major junction point that operates 333 trains daily and joins the Hempstead Branch to the Main Line. Upcoming work will require service modifications for three weekends.

CBTC signal modernization on the Fulton and Liberty Lines on the A and C in Brooklyn and Queens. Covers 42.7 track miles and provides full underground cellular service through a partnership with Boldyn.

Replacement of 85-year-old facility with a modern depot capable of housing 272 buses, supporting full electrification and high-voltage infrastructure. Design-build project. $649 million budget. Forecast substantial completion date of October 2027.

tool_args.label: Rockaway Line Resiliency and Viaduct Rehabilitation

payload.description: Rockaway Line resiliency and viaduct rehabilitation project; within budget with estimated completion cost of $584 million. Signal system improvements at Beach 105th Street Station have a forecast delay of approximately 10 months.
payload.project_name: Rockaway Line Resiliency and Viaduct Rehabilitation

tool_args.label: Miriam Harris, Senior Vice President of Transit Oriented Development
tool_args.raw_text: Ms. Miriam Harris, Senior Vice President of Transit Oriented Development (TOD), presented highlights of the unit's activities

payload.title: Senior Vice President of Transit Oriented Development

tool_args.label: Painting and structural steel repairs on elevated structures - Jamaica Line and Canarsie Line

payload.description: Paint removal, abrasive blasting, repainting, and necessary steel repairs along the Jamaica Line between the Williamsburg Bridge and Delancey Street, as well as the Canarsie Line. Estimated completion cost of $217 million. Substantial completion revised from March 2026 to September 2026.
payload.project_name: Painting and structural steel repairs on elevated structures - Jamaica Line and Canarsie Line

tool_args.label: Line structure component repairs - Seventh and Eighth Avenue Lines

payload.description: Design-bid-build project addressing critical defects on the Seventh and Eighth Avenue Lines through targeted steel and concrete repairs to columns, beams, and tunnel walls. Affects five Manhattan stations. Includes stormwater mitigation, demolition of abandoned structures, and repairs to girders and abutments. Substantial completion forecast for January 2028.
payload.project_name: Line structure component repairs - Seventh and Eighth Avenue Lines

tool_args.label: New fare gate prototypes tested at 9 stations
tool_args.raw_text: New fare gate prototypes...three different models, all being paid for by the companies who are competing for this contract. There are three different models being tested across nine stations. That number of stations is going to double in the weeks to come. More than 2 million people have used them so far...Ultimately new faregates will be installed in 150 stations as part of our 2025-2029 Capital Program. The selected stations will account for over 70% of MTA ridership.

payload.claim_text: New fare gate prototypes are getting a lot of attention. Three different models being tested across nine stations. That number of stations is going to double in the weeks to come. More than 2 million people have used them so far. Ultimately new faregates will be installed in 150 stations as part of our 2025-2029 Capital Program. The selected stations will account for over 70% of MTA ridership.

tool_args.label: Westchester Bee-Line Bus System on OMNY
tool_args.raw_text: Board Member Lopez thanked President Torres-Springer and his staff for his presentation and for their help in bringing the West Chester Bee-Line Bus System onto the OMNY system.

payload.claim_text: Board Member Lopez thanked President Torres-Springer and his staff for their help in bringing the West Chester Bee-Line Bus System onto the OMNY system.

tool_args.label: MTA exploring Q West project
tool_args.raw_text: MTA will take a serious look into the possibility of keeping the tunnel boring machine moving when it reaches the end of its path in this phase of the Second Avenue Subway...to see if it may be continued west, perhaps all the way to Broadway...MTA is calling the Q West project, Q to the west – across 125th Street.

payload.claim_text: MTA will take a serious look into the possibility of keeping the tunnel boring machine moving when it reaches the end of its path in this phase of the Second Avenue Subway. MTA will look to see if it may be continued west, perhaps all the way to Broadway to lay the core groundwork for the next phase of Second Avenue Subway. MTA is not committing financially to a project however MTA will explore it and the opportunity to explain what MTA is calling the Q West project, Q to the west across 125th Street.
